description_long: This object is a long, straight-bladed sword with a rectangular cross-section and a pointed tip, reminiscent of a European longsword or greatsword. The blade appears to be forged from a metallic material, exhibiting a slightly matte, gray finish with subtle linear texture along its length. The crossguard is simple and straight, extending horizontally to provide hand protection, and is visually distinct from the blade and handle. The handle is cylindrical, slightly darker in color, and appears to be constructed from a different material, possibly wood or wrapped metal, with no visible ergonomic shaping or decorative features. The pommel is minimal and blends seamlessly with the handle, lacking any prominent embellishments. Overall, the sword has a utilitarian, functional appearance with no visible ornamentation or sheath.
description: A long, straight-bladed sword with a simple crossguard and a plain, cylindrical handle. The finish is matte gray with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the straight blade, crossguard, and cylindrical handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin edge of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, plain, possibly wood or wrapped metal, no visible ergonomic features
